---
name: simulate-user
description: |
  Drive the REAL Leadbay MCP server as a synthetic paying customer, so the
  product team can generate true production-shaped telemetry and discover where
  the agent + tools fall short. Each run picks an observed customer persona
  (from the fr_prod workflow study), plays that persona's multi-day sales chase
  as a multi-turn `claude -p` conversation against the live API, and — because
  it runs the production server with telemetry ON — every `leadbay_*` tool call
  fires a real PostHog event that lands in the leadbay/mcp-dashboard exactly
  like a customer session. A local trace + friction report capture what the
  agent tried, what the tools could and could NOT do, and which observed
  write-back gaps (notes, epilogue, buy-contact, export) the MCP still cannot
  serve.

  This is NOT /eval. /eval scores a single WORKFLOWS.md contract for
  pass/fail correctness with a blind judge. /simulate-user reproduces a whole
  human's realistic behaviour to PRODUCE data and surface gaps — there is no
  pass/fail, the deliverable is the trace + the friction findings + the PostHog
  rows.

  For fully unattended runs (no approval prompts), launch Claude Code with:
    claude --dangerously-skip-permissions

  Usage:
    /simulate-user                         (picks a persona at random, 1 session)
    /simulate-user --persona telesales-chaser
    /simulate-user --persona founder-buyer --turns 6
    /simulate-user --persona triager --runs 3
    /simulate-user --list                  (print the persona catalog and exit)
    /simulate-user --dry-run               (build the prompts, do NOT call the API or emit telemetry)

  Triggers: "/simulate-user", "simulate a user", "simulate an MCP user",
  "fake user session", "generate MCP data", "play a persona", "synthetic user"
version: 0.1.0
allowed-tools:
  - Bash
  - Read
  - Agent
  - Skill
---

## Preamble (run first)

```bash
# Update check
_UPD=$(~/.leadbay-skills/bin/lb-skills-update-check 2>/dev/null || true)
[ -n "$_UPD" ] && echo "$_UPD" || true
```

If output shows `UPGRADE_AVAILABLE <old> <new>`: tell the user
"Leadbay skills update available: v{old} -> v{new}. Run `~/.leadbay-skills/setup --update`"
and continue with the skill.
## Voice

Direct, concrete, no filler. Sound like a builder reporting to builders.
Name specific people, specific numbers. Skip generic praise.
If something is noteworthy, say exactly what and why.
## Completion Status Protocol

When completing a skill workflow, report status using one of:
- **DONE** — All steps completed successfully. Evidence provided for each claim.
- **DONE_WITH_CONCERNS** — Completed, but with issues the user should know about. List each concern.
- **BLOCKED** — Cannot proceed. State what is blocking and what was tried.
- **NEEDS_CONTEXT** — Missing information required to continue. State exactly what you need.

### Escalation

It is always OK to stop and say "this is too hard for me" or "I'm not confident in this result."

Bad work is worse than no work. You will not be penalized for escalating.
- If you have attempted a task 3 times without success, STOP and escalate.
- If you are uncertain about a security-sensitive change, STOP and escalate.
- If the scope of work exceeds what you can verify, STOP and escalate.

Escalation format:
```
STATUS: BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
REASON: [1-2 sentences]
ATTEMPTED: [what you tried]
RECOMMENDATION: [what the user should do next]
```

# /simulate-user — Synthetic Leadbay MCP customer

Reproduce a real engaged customer's sales motion as a live MCP session so the
team can (1) generate production-shaped telemetry in the mcp-dashboard and
(2) see exactly where today's tools fail the workflow.

Every persona below is a **directly observed trace** from the fr_prod
`user_interactions` study (`Downloads/observed_workflows_report.html`) — not an
invented persona. The recurring product finding the study surfaced: prospecting
is a multi-day telephone/field chase logged in Leadbay as a tight write-back
loop, and **every chain terminates in a write the MCP cannot perform today**
(create_lead_note, epilogue_*, purchase_lead_contact, bulk export). The point of
this skill is to make that gap show up in real data, repeatedly, on demand.

---

## The Iron Laws

These rules are inviolable.

**Iron Law #1 — It must be a REAL interaction.**
The whole value is production-shaped telemetry. Run the **production** MCP server
(`packages/mcp` bin / `buildServer` with a real telemetry handle), against the
**live** Leadbay API, with telemetry **ON**. Never mock the API, never use the
`NOOP_TELEMETRY` eval helper for a recorded run, never fabricate a PostHog row.
A simulated run that did not actually hit the API produced no data — say so.

**Iron Law #2 — Sims use a DEDICATED sim account, never a real customer's token.**
PostHog `distinctId` is the account's `me.email` and events are grouped by the
account's `organization`. If you run a sim under a real customer's token you
poison that customer's dashboard. ALWAYS use the sim token from `.env.simulate`
(`LEADBAY_SIM_TOKEN`), whose account exists only for simulation.

**Iron Law #3 — Tag every sim so it is filterable out of true prod.**
Always export `LEADBAY_ENV=simulation` for the server process. This stamps
`environment=simulation` on every PostHog event so dashboard queries can include
OR exclude sims deliberately. A sim that looks identical to real prod traffic is
worse than no sim.

**Iron Law #4 — Personas are observed, not invented.**
Persona behaviour (cadence, action mix, the French call-note style, the
write-back loop) comes from the study. Do not add a motion no observed user
performed. New personas require a new observed trace.

**Iron Law #5 — Report gaps honestly.**
When the agent wanted to write a note / set an epilogue / buy a contact / bulk
export and no MCP tool existed, that is the headline finding — record it as a
GAP in the friction report, do not paper over it by having the agent pretend it
succeeded. The gap is the deliverable.

**Iron Law #6 — No silent phase failures.**
If any bash command in a phase exits non-zero, print the error and stop that
run. Report it as a skill execution error, not as "the persona finished".

---

## How this differs from /eval

| | `/eval` | `/simulate-user` |
|---|---|---|
| Goal | Score correctness of ONE workflow contract | Produce realistic data + find tool gaps |
| Server | eval `live-mcp-server.ts` (telemetry **OFF**, NOOP) | **production** server, telemetry **ON** |
| Telemetry | suppressed (clean eval) | **fires real PostHog → mcp-dashboard** |
| Output | MM/IA/NF/TSF scorecard, pass/fail | trace JSONL + friction report + PostHog rows |
| Judge | blind judge subagent | none — there is no pass/fail |
| Account | the eval token | a **dedicated sim token** |

If the user actually wants a pass/fail score, route them to `/eval`.

---

## Phase 0 — Parse arguments, locate repo, load the sim credentials

```bash
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)
echo "REPO_ROOT: $REPO_ROOT"
ls "$REPO_ROOT/packages/mcp/test/eval/helpers/live-mcp-server.ts" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
  && echo "LEADCLAW_REPO: OK" || echo "LEADCLAW_REPO: NOT the leadclaw repo — cd there first"
```

Parse the user's message for:
- `--persona <id>` — one of the catalog ids in Phase 1 (default: random).
- `--runs N` — how many independent sessions to play (default: 1).
- `--turns N` — override the persona's default turn count.
- `--list` — print the persona catalog (Phase 1 table) and exit.
- `--dry-run` — build the turn prompts and print them, but do NOT spawn the
  session, do NOT hit the API, do NOT emit telemetry. For inspecting prompts.
- `--model M` — model override for the session (default: inherit).

Load the **sim** credentials (NOT the eval token, NOT a customer token):

```bash
set -a && . "$REPO_ROOT/.env.simulate" && set +a 2>/dev/null || true
echo "SIM_TOKEN: ${LEADBAY_SIM_TOKEN:0:8}... (${#LEADBAY_SIM_TOKEN} chars)"
echo "SIM_REGION: ${LEADBAY_SIM_REGION:-fr}"
```

If `LEADBAY_SIM_TOKEN` is empty or < 10 chars: STOP and tell the user to create
`.env.simulate` at the repo root:

```
LEADBAY_SIM_TOKEN=u.xxxxx        # a token for a DEDICATED simulation account, never a real customer
LEADBAY_SIM_REGION=fr            # the study ran on fr_prod; fr keeps personas realistic
```

> **Why a dedicated account (Iron Law #2):** every tool call this skill makes is
> attributed in PostHog to the token's account email and org. A sim under a real
> customer's token corrupts that customer's funnel. The sim account is
> expendable and exists only to generate this data.

**Unattended mode:** this skill spawns subagents and runs the CLI. If Claude
Code prompts for approval on each action, tell the user to relaunch with
`claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`.

---

## Phase 1 — The persona catalog (observed rhythms)

Each persona is one of the eight observed rhythm segments. `--list` prints this
table. Pick the requested `--persona`, or choose one at random.

| id | Observed rhythm | Dominant motion (W-id) | Action mix | Default turns | The gap it exercises |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `telesales-chaser` | Bursty weekday telesales chaser (Axe-Capital / HOME SPIRIT model) | W1 master loop: buy → call → note → epilogue, re-touched over days | buy-contact, create_lead_note ("NRP"), epilogue_still_chasing / could_not_reach | 6 | note-write + epilogue + buy-contact are ALL uncovered writes |
| `founder-buyer` | Steady / burst contact-buyer (founder-SDR: etienne, jeangabriel) | W3 enrich shortlist → buy → export; outcomes via next-morning epilogue | research, purchase_lead_contact, export, set-epilogue | 5 | buy + export + set-epilogue gaps |
| `triager` | Frequent daily triager (calligaris, Cubik, BAFA) | W2 curate the feed by thumbs + lens tuning | bulk like/dislike, lens_filter_change, occasional research | 4 | committing like/dislike/lens-change is an uncovered write |
| `dataops-admin` | Bulk-export / data-ops admin (nlevasseur, sjanson, Logiprox) | W4 harvest whole key-account families' contacts in one click | research, bulk contact harvest/export, T0–T9 tier notes | 4 | programmatic export_account_contacts is missing |
| `batch-export-team` | Coordinated territory batch-export wave (Actis, manager-orchestrated) | W5 each rep bulk-exports a FIXED territory allocation to an external dialer | pull/shortlist, fixed-size export | 4 | the entire motion is write-back export the MCP can't do |
| `snooze-builder` | Research-then-snooze list-builder (brelvictor / TRANSPORTS BREL) | W6 like → web-research → pushback_lead_6 to defer 6 months | like, research_lead, bulk-snooze-with-reason | 4 | bulk-snooze-with-reason is uncovered |
| `field-logger` | Field / offline-to-online logger (Logiprox, LAVINIA) | W7 real value happens off-platform, back-logged as dense FR call recaps | buy-contact, create_lead_note (long recaps), epilogue | 5 | every step (note + epilogue + buy + export) is a write gap |
| `bot-pusher` | True daily / bot-paced operator (aurelien @ Vocca) | W8 bulk buy-contact at machine cadence → "Pushed to Attio" note → CRM | rapid purchase_lead_contact, machine note, push-to-CRM | 5 | programmatic buy-contact + push-to-CRM write API is missing |

The W-ids map to the observed workflow catalog (W1–W11) in the study. Personas
deliberately drive the agent toward the write-back steps where the gaps live —
that is how the gaps show up in the data.

---

## Phase 2 — Build the persona's turn script

A persona session is a **multi-turn** conversation: the user opens with their
real morning intent, then re-touches the same leads over "days" the way the
study shows. Construct an ordered `TURN_PROMPTS` array in the persona's own
voice (the study's reps are French SDRs — keep the call-note phrasing FR where
the persona is FR, e.g. "NRP", "le rappeler vers 14h", "a déjà un prestataire").

Ground rules for authoring turns:
- **Open with the natural intent**, not a tool name. ("Montre-moi mes leads à
  rappeler aujourd'hui", "Pull my best new prospects this morning.")
- **Drive toward the write-back step** the persona's gap exercises. When the
  agent reaches the wall (no tool to write a note / set epilogue / buy / export),
  let it — that wall is the finding. Do not coach the agent around it.
- **Re-touch across turns** for the chase personas (telesales-chaser,
  field-logger): turn 1 = first dial + log; turn 2 = "called back, still no
  answer"; turn 3 = "reached them, booked / lost". Mirror the W1c/W1d/W1f loop.
- **Stay observed.** Use the real company/note patterns from the study as
  flavour (HOTEL DE LA CROIX ROUSSE, "NRP n", scheduled-callback notes), never a
  motion no user performed.

Example — `telesales-chaser` (6 turns):

```
T1: "Bonjour. Montre-moi mes leads à rappeler aujourd'hui — je commence ma session d'appels."
T2: "OK je prends le premier hôtel. Achète-moi le contact direct du décideur."
T3: "Je viens d'appeler, NRP (pas de réponse). Note-le et garde-le en cours de relance."
T4: "Passons au suivant — j'ai eu la standardiste, elle me dit de rappeler vers 14h. Note ça."
T5: "Rappelé celui de ce matin : toujours NRP, 3e tentative. Mets-le en 'could not reach, still trying'."
T6: "Le dernier : j'ai eu le directeur, intéressé, RDV jeudi. Marque-le gagné / meeting planned."
```

Print the full `TURN_PROMPTS` for the chosen persona before running. If
`--dry-run`, stop here after printing.

---

## Phase 3 — Run the live session against the PRODUCTION server (telemetry ON)

Spawn a **session subagent** via the Agent tool. Its only job: run the
multi-turn `claude -p` conversation against the **production** MCP server with
telemetry enabled, and return the raw stream-json for every turn.

---
**SESSION SUBAGENT PROMPT:**

You are running a live Leadbay MCP **simulation** session. Run a multi-turn
`claude -p` conversation against the real Leadbay API using the PRODUCTION MCP
server with telemetry ON, and return the full stdout (stream-json) for each
turn. Do not judge or score — just capture.

**Setup:**

```bash
REPO_ROOT=REPO_ROOT_PLACEHOLDER
set -a && . "$REPO_ROOT/.env.simulate" && set +a

TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d /tmp/lb-sim-XXXXXX)
TSX=$(find "$REPO_ROOT/node_modules" -name "tsx" -path "*/.bin/tsx" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
echo "TSX: $TSX"
```

Write a tiny launcher that boots the **production** server with a **real
telemetry handle** (this is what makes events reach PostHog — the eval
`live-mcp-server.ts` deliberately omits telemetry and must NOT be used here):

```bash
cat > "$TMPDIR/sim-mcp-server.ts" << 'TSEOF'
// Production server + real telemetry → every leadbay_* call emits a PostHog event.
// NOTE: everything runs inside async main() — NO top-level await. tsx transpiles
// a bare .ts to CJS, and CJS rejects top-level await ("Top-level await is
// currently not supported with the cjs output format"), so a top-level
// `await server.connect(...)` makes the server fail to boot and the MCP shows
// status:"failed" with zero tool calls. The eval live-mcp-server.ts uses the
// same async-main shape for this reason.
import { StdioServerTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js";
import { LeadbayClient } from "@leadbay/core";
import { buildServer } from "REPO_ROOT_PLACEHOLDER/packages/mcp/src/server.js";
import { initTelemetry } from "REPO_ROOT_PLACEHOLDER/packages/mcp/src/telemetry.js";

const REGIONS: Record<string, string> = {
  us: "https://api-us.leadbay.app",
  fr: "https://api-fr.leadbay.app",
};
async function main(): Promise<void> {
  const token = process.env.LEADBAY_TOKEN!;
  const region = process.env.LEADBAY_REGION ?? "fr";
  const client = new LeadbayClient(REGIONS[region] ?? REGIONS.fr, token);
  // Telemetry handle: initTelemetry honours LEADBAY_TELEMETRY_ENABLED + LEADBAY_ENV.
  const telemetry = initTelemetry({ version: "sim" });
  try { await telemetry.identify(client); } catch {}   // tags events with me.email + org
  const server = buildServer(client, { includeWrite: true, includeAdvanced: false, telemetry });
  const transport = new StdioServerTransport();
  await server.connect(transport);
}
main().catch((err) => { process.stderr.write(`sim-mcp-server fatal: ${err}\n`); process.exit(1); });
TSEOF
sed -i "s#REPO_ROOT_PLACEHOLDER#$REPO_ROOT#g" "$TMPDIR/sim-mcp-server.ts"
```

> The production `buildServer` + `initTelemetry` path is the same one
> `packages/mcp/src/bin.ts` uses in a real install. `initTelemetry` returns the
> live PostHog handle UNLESS `LEADBAY_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false` or
> `NODE_ENV=test`. We set neither off — so telemetry is live. `identify(client)`
> resolves the account email so `distinctId` is correct (Iron Law #2).

Write the MCP config — note the env stamps that satisfy Iron Laws #2 and #3:

```bash
cat > "$TMPDIR/mcp-config.json" << MCPEOF
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "leadbay": {
      "command": "$TSX",
      "args": ["$TMPDIR/sim-mcp-server.ts"],
      "env": {
        "LEADBAY_TOKEN": "$LEADBAY_SIM_TOKEN",
        "LEADBAY_REGION": "${LEADBAY_SIM_REGION:-fr}",
        "LEADBAY_TELEMETRY_ENABLED": "true",
        "LEADBAY_ENV": "simulation"
      }
    }
  }
}
MCPEOF
```

> `LEADBAY_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=true` forces the PostHog handle on; `LEADBAY_ENV=simulation`
> stamps `environment=simulation` on every event so the dashboard can include or
> exclude sims (Iron Law #3). Do NOT set `NODE_ENV=test` — that short-circuits
> `initTelemetry` to NOOP and you would get zero data.

Clean settings (no hooks, no plugins):

```bash
cat > "$TMPDIR/settings.json" << 'SETEOF'
{ "hooks": { "PreToolUse": [], "PostToolUse": [], "UserPromptSubmit": [], "SessionStart": [], "Stop": [] }, "enabledPlugins": {} }
SETEOF
```

System prompt — always pass one (the project CLAUDE.md otherwise restricts
Claude to software-engineering tasks and it refuses Leadbay tools). Use the
persona-flavoured assistant prompt:

```bash
EFFECTIVE_SYSTEM_PROMPT="You are the Leadbay sales assistant inside a French B2B SDR's chat. Use the available Leadbay MCP tools to help them work their leads by phone — pull follow-ups, research, buy contacts, log call outcomes. Respond in the user's language. When a tool you need does not exist, say so plainly rather than pretending the action succeeded."
```

**Multi-turn run** — one session id, turn 1 sets `--session-id`, later turns
`--resume`. The MCP server restarts per turn so the 10s handshake delay applies
to each turn (lets the tsx server finish its stdio handshake before the first
turn — without it the session starts `pending` and tool calls fail):

```bash
SESSION_ID=$(cat /proc/sys/kernel/random/uuid)
START_MS=$(date +%s%3N)
TURN_IDX=0
for TURN_PROMPT in "${TURN_PROMPTS[@]}"; do
  TURN_IDX=$((TURN_IDX + 1))
  TURN_RAW="$TMPDIR/session.turn${TURN_IDX}.raw.jsonl"
  USER_MSG=$(printf '{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"%s"}}' \
    "$(echo "$TURN_PROMPT" | sed 's/"/\\"/g')")
  if [ "$TURN_IDX" -eq 1 ]; then SESSION_FLAG=(--session-id "$SESSION_ID")
  else SESSION_FLAG=(--resume "$SESSION_ID"); fi

  { sleep 10; echo "$USER_MSG"; } | claude -p \
    --input-format stream-json --output-format stream-json --verbose \
    "${SESSION_FLAG[@]}" \
    --mcp-config "$TMPDIR/mcp-config.json" --settings "$TMPDIR/settings.json" \
    --dangerously-skip-permissions --strict-mcp-config --disable-slash-commands \
    --allowedTools "mcp__leadbay__*,ask_user_input_v0" \
    --disallowedTools "ToolSearch,WebFetch,WebSearch,Bash,Read,Edit,Write,Glob,Grep,LS,Skill,LSP,Agent" \
    --system-prompt "$EFFECTIVE_SYSTEM_PROMPT" \
    ${MODEL:+--model "$MODEL"} \
    > "$TURN_RAW" 2>"$TMPDIR/stderr.turn${TURN_IDX}.txt"
  echo "=== TURN $TURN_IDX (exit $?) ==="; cat "$TURN_RAW"; echo "=== END TURN $TURN_IDX ==="
done
END_MS=$(date +%s%3N); DURATION_MS=$((END_MS - START_MS))
echo "DURATION_MS: $DURATION_MS  TURNS: $TURN_IDX  SESSION_ID: $SESSION_ID"
# Give PostHog's async flush time to ship before the tsx server is torn down.
sleep 12
rm -rf "$TMPDIR"
```

Replace `REPO_ROOT_PLACEHOLDER` with the actual `$REPO_ROOT`. Set `TURN_PROMPTS`
(from Phase 2) and `MODEL` from the persona/flags.

From the stream-json output, extract (across ALL turns) and RETURN:
- **tool_calls**: `type=assistant` blocks with `tool_use` → `name` + `input` +
  the 1-based turn index. Keep only `mcp__leadbay__*` / `leadbay_*` names plus
  any `ask_user_input_v0`. (Other names are leaked superpowers — drop them.)
- **agent_prose**: text blocks that are NOT tool_use — especially any line where
  the agent says a capability is missing ("I can't write a note", "there's no
  tool to set the status").
- **final_message**: the `type=result` field of each turn.
- **errors**: any tool result with `is_error` / a non-ok envelope.
- **SESSION_ID** and **DURATION_MS**.

Return all of the above as structured JSON. Do not assess quality.

---
**END SESSION SUBAGENT PROMPT**

If `--dry-run` was set, skip this phase entirely (you already printed the turns).

---

## Phase 4 — Build the trace + friction report

From the session subagent's returned evidence, assemble two artifacts under
`$REPO_ROOT/.context/sims/`.

```bash
mkdir -p "$REPO_ROOT/.context/sims"
STAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
TRACE="$REPO_ROOT/.context/sims/${STAMP}_${PERSONA_ID}.trace.json"
REPORT="$REPO_ROOT/.context/sims/${STAMP}_${PERSONA_ID}.friction.md"
```

**Trace JSON** (`*.trace.json`) — the machine record:

```json
{
  "persona": "telesales-chaser",
  "rhythm": "Bursty weekday telesales chaser",
  "session_id": "...",
  "region": "fr",
  "environment": "simulation",
  "duration_ms": 0,
  "turns": [
    { "turn": 1, "user": "...", "tool_calls": [{"name":"leadbay_pull_followups","input":{}}], "agent_prose": "...", "result": "..." }
  ],
  "tool_call_summary": { "leadbay_pull_followups": 1, "leadbay_research_lead_by_id": 2 },
  "gaps": [ { "wanted": "write a call note", "observed_motif": "W1b create_lead_note", "mcp_tool": null, "agent_said": "I don't have a tool to save that note." } ]
}
```

**Friction report** (`*.friction.md`) — the human read. Structure:

```
# Sim: <persona> — <STAMP>

**Session:** <session_id> · region <fr> · environment=simulation · <duration>s
**Telemetry:** real PostHog events emitted under <sim account email> (see Phase 5).

## What the persona did
<2–4 sentences narrating the turns in plain language.>

## Tools the agent actually called
<table: tool · count · did it return ok?>

## GAPS — where the workflow hit a wall  ← the headline
For each write-back the agent wanted but could not perform:
- **<wanted action>** (observed motif <W-id>): no MCP tool. Agent said: "<quote>".
  Observed-customer equivalent: <one line from the study, e.g. "299 instances of create_lead_note → epilogue_still_chasing across 8 users">.

## Friction (covered-but-clumsy)
<Where a tool exists but the routing/shape made the agent struggle — e.g. it
researched the wrong lead, the table was unreadable, it asked a redundant
clarification.>

## Suggested product follow-up
<1–3 concrete tool/prompt ideas the gap implies — e.g. a log_call_outcome(note,
epilogue) write tool owning W1b. Phrase as hypotheses, not commitments.>
```

The GAPS section is the deliverable (Iron Law #5). If the agent sailed through
with no gap, say so explicitly — that is also a finding (the motion is already
covered).

---

## Phase 5 — Confirm the telemetry actually landed

The run is only valuable if it produced real dashboard data. Verify the events
reached PostHog (this is also the integration with the mcp-dashboard — the same
project these events feed).

## PostHog Setup

Resolve the key from the environment first (so unattended/CI runs can supply it
as a secret), then fall back to the saved config file:

```bash
_PH_KEY="${PH_KEY:-${POSTHOG_API_KEY:-}}"
[ -z "$_PH_KEY" ] && _PH_KEY=$(~/.leadbay-skills/bin/lb-skills-config get posthog_api_key 2>/dev/null || echo "")
echo "PH_KEY_SET: $([ -n "$_PH_KEY" ] && echo yes || echo no)"
```

If `PH_KEY_SET` is `no` AND this is an interactive run: Ask the user for their
PostHog personal API key. Say: "I need a PostHog personal API key to query user
analytics. Generate one at https://eu.posthog.com/settings/user-api-keys (read
access is sufficient)." In a non-interactive / `--auto` run there is no one to
ask — if the key is absent, fail the Phase 0 gate loudly and stop.

Once provided, save it:
```bash
~/.leadbay-skills/bin/lb-skills-config set posthog_api_key "<THE_KEY>"
```

**PostHog config:**
```
POSTHOG_HOST=https://eu.posthog.com
```

All HogQL queries use: `POST $POSTHOG_HOST/api/projects/@current/query/`
with header `Authorization: Bearer $PH_KEY` and body `{"query": {"kind": "HogQLQuery", "query": "..."}}`

Query for the events this session just emitted, filtered to the sim environment
so you do not scan all of prod:

```bash
SINCE_ISO=$(date -u -d '20 minutes ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ 2>/dev/null || date -u -v-20M +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
read -r -d '' HOGQL <<SQL || true
SELECT event, count() AS n, max(timestamp) AS last_seen
FROM events
WHERE timestamp > toDateTime('${SINCE_ISO}')
  AND properties.environment = 'simulation'
GROUP BY event
ORDER BY n DESC
SQL
curl -s -X POST "$POSTHOG_HOST/api/projects/@current/query/" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $_PH_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "$(printf '{"query":{"kind":"HogQLQuery","query":%s}}' "$(printf '%s' "$HOGQL" | jq -Rs .)")" | jq .
```

Report:
- `[✓] N PostHog events landed under environment=simulation in the last 20 min` —
  matching the `leadbay_*` tool calls in the trace, OR
- `[✗] 0 events landed` → telemetry did NOT fire. Most likely cause: the run used
  the eval `live-mcp-server.ts` (NOOP) instead of the production launcher, or
  `LEADBAY_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false` / `NODE_ENV=test` was set, or the PostHog
  flush was torn down before the `sleep 12`. State which and that the run
  produced NO dashboard data (Iron Law #1).

> The mcp-dashboard (leadbay/mcp-dashboard, Fly) reads these same PostHog
> events. Once landed, the sim session appears there alongside real customer
> sessions, distinguishable by `environment=simulation`. No extra push step is
> needed — the dashboard is downstream of PostHog.

---

## Phase 6 — Loop for `--runs N` and summarize

If `--runs N` > 1, repeat Phases 2–5 N times (re-pick a random persona each run
if no `--persona` was pinned; keep the same persona if it was). Write one
trace + friction file per run.

Final summary to the user:
- personas played, turns each, total `leadbay_*` calls,
- total PostHog events confirmed landed (with `environment=simulation`),
- the **distinct gaps** found across all runs (deduped), ranked by how many
  observed users the study attributes to that motion,
- where to look: `.context/sims/*.friction.md` locally + the mcp-dashboard
  filtered to `environment=simulation`.

Close with the completion status (DONE / DONE_WITH_CONCERNS / BLOCKED).

---

## Operating notes

- **This is data generation, not testing.** There is no pass/fail. A run that
  surfaces a clean gap is a *successful* run even though the agent "failed" to
  complete the persona's motion — because the gap is the point.
- **Keep personas observed.** When the study is updated with new traces, add the
  new rhythm to the Phase 1 catalog; never invent a persona from intuition
  (Iron Law #4).
- **Dedicated account hygiene.** The sim account will accumulate liked/disliked
  leads, bought contacts, and lens changes over many runs. That is expected and
  is itself realistic ageing — but it means the sim account must NEVER be a real
  customer. Re-confirm `.env.simulate` points at the sim account before any run.
- **If the user wants a score, not data**, route to `/eval`.
- **Known live friction (first run, 2026-06-17, telesales-chaser):** the W1
  write-back gap the study reported as "no MCP tool" is now partly closed —
  `leadbay_report_outreach` exists and the agent routes to it correctly (note +
  the right epilogue enum). BUT in a non-interactive session every call was
  blocked: it returned *"User cancelled the outreach confirmation; nothing was
  logged"* and the only escape the error names is a `gmail_message_id` /
  `calendar_event_id` a cold phone call doesn't have. So the disposition write —
  including the W1g meeting-planned money event — could not complete agentically.
  Expect this; it is itself a headline finding, not a harness bug. (The harness
  bug was the top-level-await launcher, now fixed above.)

## Operational Self-Improvement

Before completing, reflect on this session:
- Did any commands fail unexpectedly?
- Did you take a wrong approach and have to backtrack?
- Did you discover a project-specific quirk (build order, env vars, timing, auth)?
- Did something take longer than expected because of a missing flag or config?

If yes, log an operational learning for future sessions:

```bash
"$_LB_BIN/lb-learnings-log" '{"skill":"SKILL_NAME","type":"operational","key":"SHORT_KEY","insight":"DESCRIPTION","confidence":N,"source":"observed"}'
```

Replace SKILL_NAME with the current skill name. Only log genuine operational discoveries.
Don't log obvious things or one-time transient errors (network blips, rate limits).
A good test: would knowing this save 5+ minutes in a future session? If yes, log it.
